title: OS Process Scheduling
description: Operating system schedulers use priority queues (often implemented as binary heaps) to select the next process to run. Higher-priority or shorter-deadline processes are extracted in O(log n) time, ensuring responsive scheduling even with thousands of runnable processes.

title: Event-Driven Simulation
description: Discrete event simulators maintain a min-heap of future events ordered by their scheduled timestamp. The simulation loop repeatedly extracts the earliest event, processes it, and may insert new events — the heap guarantees correct temporal ordering with O(log n) insert and extract operations.

title: K-Way Merge
description: External sort algorithms merge multiple sorted runs (from disk) using a min-heap of size k holding the smallest unprocessed element from each run. The heap enables selecting the global minimum in O(log k) time, producing the final sorted output in a single streaming pass.
